description: Error handling patterns for Rust - Result/Option abuse, unwrap misuse, error propagation

rules.name: Unwrap on Option Should Be Avoided
rules.description: Using unwrap() on Option<T> can cause panic at runtime. Use unwrap_or, unwrap_or_else, or pattern matching instead.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect method_call with method name 'unwrap' where the receiver is of type Option. AST query: (method_call function: (identifier) @method (#eq? @method "unwrap"))
false_positive_guards: unwrap on Result is sometimes acceptable, only flag unwrap on Option

rules.name: Expect on Option Should Be Avoided
rules.description: Using expect() on Option<T> can cause panic with an unwitnessed message. Use unwrap_or_else with a meaningful fallback or proper error handling.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect method_call with method name 'expect' where the receiver is of type Option. AST query similar to CC_ERR_001 but for 'expect'.
false_positive_guards: unwrap_or_else is preferred over expect for Option

rules.name: Unwrap on Result Should Provide Context
rules.description: Using unwrap() on Result<T, E> loses error information. Use unwrap_err(), unwrap_unchecked(), or proper error handling with ? operator.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect method_call with method name 'unwrap' on Result. AST: (method_call function: (field_expression field: (identifier) @field (#eq? @field "unwrap")))

rules.name: Expect on Result Should Include Error Context
rules.description: expect() message should describe the error context, not just repeat 'called Result::unwrap() on an Err value'.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect method_call with 'expect' on Result, check if the argument is a meaningful string (not the default message).
false_positive_guards: expect with custom message is acceptable

rules.name: Error Chain Should Be Propagated With ? Operator
rules.description: When catching an error and returning it, use ? instead of match or if let Some to preserve the error chain.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect function calls that wrap error in Box<dyn Error> or similar without using ?, pattern: return Err(X.into()) instead of return Err(X)?

rules.name: Custom Error Type Should Implement std::error::Error
rules.description: Custom error types should implement std::error::Error trait for proper error handling and debugging.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect struct definitions that derive Error but don't implement std::error::Error. Look for #[derive(Error)] without impl Error for struct.
false_positive_guards: enum errors with thiserror are usually fine

rules.name: Result Should Be Used Instead of Option for Error Handling
rules.description: When a function can fail, use Result<T, E> instead of Option<T> to provide error context.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect functions returning Option where the None case represents an error condition. Look for functions with Option return that access external resources.
false_positive_guards: Some Option uses are legitimate (e.g., cache misses without error)

rules.name: Panic Should Not Be Used for Validation
rules.description: Using panic! or unwrap() for input validation is inappropriate. Use Result or explicit validation with clear error messages.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect panic! macro calls in functions that take public parameters. AST: (macro_invocation macro: (identifier) @macro (#eq? @macro "panic"))
false_positive_guards: Panic in tests is acceptable, panic in unreachable!() is fine

rules.name: unwrap_or_default Should Provide Meaningful Default
rules.description: Using unwrap_or_default() can hide errors by providing a meaningless default value like 0 or empty string.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect method_call 'unwrap_or_default' on Result or Option.
false_positive_guards: For primitive types where default is meaningful (e.g., Vec::new())

rules.name: match Should Handle All Variants or Use _
rules.description: Incomplete matches on Result or Option can panic. Always handle all variants or use _ wildcard.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect match expressions on Result/Option that don't cover all variants. AST: (match_expression arm pattern (omitted_spread))

rules.name: Error Should Be Logged Before Converting
rules.description: When converting errors (e.g., from io::Error to custom error), log the original error before discarding information.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect ? operator on Result without prior log::error or similar logging call in the same function.
false_positive_guards: Library code should not log; application code should

rules.name: to_string Should Not Be Used for Error Conversion
rules.description: Using to_string() on errors loses type information. Use from() or into() for proper error conversion.
rules.detection_strategy: Detect .to_string() call where the context suggests error conversion (e.g., Err(x.to_string()))
false_positive_guards: to_string for display purposes is fine

research_notes: Based on SonarQube SonarRust analyzer rules for error handling
All rules adapted for Creative Commons BY-ND 3.0 reference license
Detection strategies use tree-sitter Rust grammar
Rust-specific patterns due to Result/Option type system
